data.title: General Mills Issues Soft Outlook, Restructuring Costs Soar To $165 Million
data.description:
General Mills (NYSE:GIS) shares are trading lower premarket on Tuesday as the company outlines its progress on its Accelerate strategy, which aims to drive long-term sustainable growth.

Details

During its presentation at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York (CAGNY) Conference, General Mills highlighted its commitment to restoring organic sales growth through its Accelerate strategy.

The company’s focus on its Accelerate strategy is significant as it aims to navigate a challenging consumer environment and drive sustainable growth.

In the presentation, CEO Jeff Harmening emphasized the company’s focus on enhancing brand competitiveness and delivering greater remarkability through its global brands.

Despite the current challenging environment, the company aims to adapt and drive profitable growth, showcasing its portfolio of well-known brands like Cheerios, Häagen-Dazs, and Blue Buffalo.

Revised Guidance

The company anticipates about a 25% increase in net sales from new products in FY26, driven by innovation around three key consumer trends: bold flavors, familiar favorites, and better-for-you benefits like protein and fiber.

General Mills is adjusting its full-year fiscal 2026 financial outlook, reflecting the challenges posed by weak consumer sentiment and heightened uncertainty in the market.

Consequently, the company has revised its full-year fiscal 2026 guidance, with organic net sales expected to be down 1.5%–2% (previously down 1% to up 1%).

Also, the company lowered its outlook for adjusted operating profit & diluted EPS (constant currency) to be down 16%–20% (previously down 10%–15%).

For full-year fiscal 2026, net sales growth is expected to be reduced by ~4% due to currency effects, recent acquisitions/divestitures, and a 53rd week.

Currency is projected to have minimal impact on adjusted profit and EPS, while restructuring and integration costs are estimated at $160–165 million.

Technical Analysis

The broader market experienced declines on the previous trading day, with the Nasdaq falling by 0.78% and the S&P 500 down 0.31%. General Mills’ positive movement contrasts with the overall market sentiment, suggesting that the stock’s performance may be driven by company-specific factors rather than broader market trends.

General Mills is currently trading 16.02% below its 12-month high, reflecting a challenging year for the stock. The shares are positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs, indicating ongoing pressure in the market.

The RSI is at a neutral level, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold at this time. Meanwhile, MACD is below its signal line, indicating bearish pressure on the stock.

The combination of neutral RSI and bearish MACD suggests mixed momentum for General Mills, indicating that traders should watch for potential shifts in market sentiment.

  • Key Resistance: $46.00
  • Key Support: $43.00

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

General Mills is slated to provide its next financial update on Mar. 18, 2026.

  • EPS Estimate: 82 cents (Down from $1.00)
  • Revenue Estimate: $4.55 Billion (Down from $4.84 Billion)
  • Valuation: P/E of 10.4x (Indicates value opportunity)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions:

The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $53.56. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Wells Fargo: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $49.00) (Jan. 5)
  • Morgan Stanley: Underweight (Lowers Target to $47.00) (Dec. 22, 2025)
  • TD Cowen: Hold (Raises Target to $47.00) (Dec. 18, 2025)

Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because GIS carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

GIS Price Action: General Mills shares are trading lower by 3.52% at $46.64 during premarket trading on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by T. Schneider via Shutterstock

data.title: Nio Smashes Battery-Swap Record In Spring Festival Rush
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle maker Nio Inc. (NYSE:NIO) reportedly set a new single-day battery swap record, driven by surging holiday travel demand across China.

The company said drivers completed 146,649 battery swaps on Feb. 15, setting a fresh operational record as Spring Festival travel intensified.

The surge highlights rising usage of its charging ecosystem, CnEV Post reports.

Holiday Travel Drives Demand

China’s Spring Festival remains the country’s busiest travel season, pushing highways and charging networks to peak activity levels.

Nio said its cumulative battery swap count crossed 100 million earlier this month, signaling broader adoption of the service model, CnEV Post adds. The company currently runs 3,750 swap stations nationwide, including more than 1,000 positioned along expressways.

Software Recall Adds Pressure

Earlier this month, the automaker initiated a recall affecting more than 246,000 vehicles after identifying a software-related safety concern.

The issue may briefly darken key display screens, according to China’s State Administration for Market Regulation.

Nio plans to deploy over-the-air updates while arranging service center upgrades when required.

The affected lineup includes ES8, ES6, and EC6 vehicles produced between March 2018 and January 2023. Despite the recall, strong usage metrics suggest customers continue relying heavily on the company’s battery swap ecosystem during peak travel periods.

NIO Price Action: Nio shares were down 0.41% at $4.92 during premarket trading on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo by Robert Way via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With JIADE Stock Tuesday?
data.description:
JIADE Limited (NASDAQ:JDZG) moved sharply lower in Tuesday’s premarket session, reversing part of last week’s rally. The stock had surged 125.44% to close at $3.81 on Friday.

The Catalyst

The decline in JDZG stock is largely tracking a broader market selloff, with the Nasdaq down 87 cents and the Technology sector declining 87 cents. This movement highlights a challenging environment for tech stocks, despite some sectors like Energy and Financials showing resilience.

Technical Analysis

Over the past 12 months, JDZG has seen a decline of 31.22%, reflecting broader market pressures and specific challenges within the tech sector.

The stock is currently trading at $2.03, which positions it significantly below its 52-week high of $6.40 and above its low of 73 cents. This range indicates volatility and a testing period for the stock’s valuation.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for JIADE, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 85.49) — The stock is outperforming the broader market in terms of momentum.

The Verdict: JIADE’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, suggesting robust market performance.

JDZG Price Action: Jiade shares were down 46.98% at $2.02 during premarket trading on Tuesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.
